International Tax Services

International Tax Advisory

Cross-border tax planning for UK businesses with international operations or subsidiaries. Our international tax specialists ensure compliance while optimizing your global tax position.

Get a QuoteBook Consultation

Global Tax Expertise

International tax matters are complex and require specialist knowledge. Our team has extensive experience helping UK businesses navigate cross-border tax issues, including transfer pricing, permanent establishment, double taxation treaties, and international structuring.

We work with a network of international tax partners to provide comprehensive support for businesses operating across multiple jurisdictions. Whether you're expanding overseas, repatriating profits, or restructuring international operations, we provide strategic advice to optimize your global tax position.

Key Considerations

  • Double taxation treaty benefits
  • Transfer pricing compliance
  • Permanent establishment risks
  • Controlled Foreign Company rules
  • Withholding tax optimization
  • Cross-border financing structures

Our International Tax Services

Cross-Border Structuring

Advisory on optimal corporate structures for international operations, including holding companies and financing arrangements.

Transfer Pricing

Transfer pricing documentation, policy development, and compliance with UK and international requirements.

Double Taxation Relief

Maximizing benefits from double taxation treaties and foreign tax credit calculations.

Controlled Foreign Companies

CFC rules compliance and structuring to avoid unintended UK tax charges on foreign subsidiaries.

Expatriate Tax

Tax planning for employees working internationally, including secondments and permanent relocations.

International Compliance

Coordination of tax filings across multiple jurisdictions and management of international deadlines.

Double Taxation Treaties

The UK has an extensive network of double taxation treaties with over 130 countries. These treaties prevent the same income from being taxed in both jurisdictions and provide reduced withholding tax rates on cross-border payments.

Treaty Benefits

  • • Reduced withholding tax on dividends
  • • Reduced withholding tax on interest
  • • Reduced withholding tax on royalties
  • • Permanent establishment protection

Key Treaty Partners

  • • United States
  • • European Union members
  • • Commonwealth countries
  • • Major Asian economies

Transfer Pricing

What is Transfer Pricing?

Transfer pricing refers to the pricing of transactions between related entities in different tax jurisdictions. Transactions must be priced at arm's length to ensure fair tax allocation.

Documentation Requirements

UK businesses with cross-border related party transactions must maintain transfer pricing documentation. Large businesses must file master file and local file documentation.

Arm's Length Principle

The arm's length principle requires related party transactions to be priced as if between independent parties. We use recognized methods including CUP, CUPM, TNMM, and profit split.

Penalties for Non-Compliance

HMRC can impose significant penalties for transfer pricing non-compliance, including adjustments to taxable profits and interest on underpaid tax. Proper documentation is essential.

Frequently Asked Questions

What is permanent establishment?

Permanent establishment is a fixed place of business through which a company carries out business in another country. Creating a PE can subject foreign profits to local tax. We help structure operations to avoid unintended PE risks.

How do double taxation treaties work?

Treaties allocate taxing rights between countries and provide relief from double taxation. They typically allow tax credits for foreign tax paid and reduce withholding tax rates on cross-border payments.

Do I need transfer pricing documentation?

If you have cross-border related party transactions, you likely need transfer pricing documentation. UK businesses with turnover over certain thresholds must file master file and local file documentation with HMRC.

Can you help with international expansion?

Yes. We advise on optimal corporate structures for international expansion, including choice of jurisdiction, subsidiary setup, and ongoing tax compliance across multiple countries.

Navigate International Tax with Confidence
